title: Chunk 0 — Source-of-Truth Docs and Stale Doc Quarantine
summary: Mark the current architecture and roadmap as authoritative, quarantine stale docs, and make the contributor reading path clear.
acceptanceCriteria: Current source-of-truth docs are listed before stale historical material.
Stale local-MVP or cloud-heavy docs contain clear warning banners or archive notes.
Root and docs README files direct new contributors to the authoritative architecture and roadmap.

title: Chunk 1 — Open-Source Foundation and Apache-2.0 Setup
summary: Add licensing, governance, issue templates, security policy, and provider-free contributor onboarding for an Apache-2.0 project.
acceptanceCriteria: Apache-2.0 license, notice, trademarks, code of conduct, contributing, and security docs are present.
Issue and pull request templates guide contributors without requiring private tools.
Provider-free quickstart explains local setup without paid service credentials.

title: Chunk 2 — Repo Migration Map and Compatibility Strategy
summary: Map old task-MVP files to the new chat/run architecture so contributors know what to preserve, wrap, rename, or retire.
acceptanceCriteria: Old task-MVP modules are inventoried against the new run/conversation architecture.
Each legacy area has a preserve, wrap, rename, or retire decision.
Tests to preserve or replace are documented for follow-up chunks.

title: Chunk 3 — Protocol Foundation
summary: Create canonical protocol contracts for phases, envelopes, events, DAG schemas, retry policies, and validation policies.
acceptanceCriteria: Protocol types cover run phases, event envelopes, DAG nodes, retry policy, and validation policy.
Invalid protocol payloads are rejected by schema tests.
No provider or UI behavior changes are introduced.

title: Chunk 4 — Core Data Model and Local Store
summary: Introduce conversation, message, run, artifact, event, and budget types with an in-memory provider-free store.
acceptanceCriteria: Core data model types exist for conversations, messages, runs, artifacts, events, and budgets.
In-memory local store supports create, read, list, update, and delete behavior needed by later chunks.
Store tests prove provider-free operation and deterministic behavior.

title: Chunk 5 — Run State Machine and Event Log
summary: Implement the hidden run lifecycle with append-only events, validated transitions, and resumable NEEDS_DECISION state.
acceptanceCriteria: Run lifecycle transitions match the architecture phase model.
Every valid transition emits an append-only event and invalid transitions are rejected.
NEEDS_DECISION is represented as a first-class resumable state.

title: Chunk 6 — Chat API Vertical Shell
summary: Expose chat-first endpoints and a minimal UI shell while keeping orchestration details behind the user-facing conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can create and open conversations through chat-first endpoints.
Sending a message produces an assistant response and visible run status metadata.
Optional trace/events visibility exists without making agent orchestration the primary UX.

title: Chunk 7 — Budget, Security, and Redaction Baseline
summary: Add local budget controls, redaction utilities, setup-checklist secret safety, CORS config, dev auth bypass, and budget denial flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Budget limits can deny work before provider calls are attempted.
Redaction utilities remove secrets from logs, telemetry, docs examples, and setup output.
Security tests cover CORS, dev-only auth bypass, rate limiting, and secret safety.

title: Chunk 8 — Triage and Context Builder
summary: Implement deterministic triage routes and context-pack generation with artifact handles for large material.
acceptanceCriteria: Incoming chat work is routed by deterministic triage rules.
Context packs include relevant artifacts, assumptions, and limits without dumping oversized content.
Large docs, logs, or repo data are referenced by artifact handles.

title: Chunk 9 — Planner Schema and Fake Planner
summary: Define planner input/output contracts and a deterministic fake planner for local tests and provider-free development.
acceptanceCriteria: Planner input and output schemas validate goals, assumptions, tasks, dependencies, validation, and risk.
Fake planner produces deterministic plans for local provider-free tests.
Approval gates are represented for unsafe or ambiguous work.

title: Chunk 10 — Skeptic Review
summary: Add adversarial plan review that flags missing validation, unsafe assumptions, unsupported claims, and nonexistent dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: Skeptic review returns structured findings with severity and actionable messages.
Review flags missing validation, dangling dependencies, risky assumptions, and unsupported claims.
Tests cover accepted plans, warnings, blockers, and low-risk underestimation.

title: Chunk 11 — Crucible Arbitration
summary: Implement deterministic rules that arbitrate planner and skeptic outputs with revision, escalation, and blocker handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Accepted plans, requested revisions, blockers, and escalation outcomes are deterministic.
Planner versus skeptic disagreements are resolved without LLM judgment.
At most two revision rounds occur before escalation.

title: Chunk 12 — DAG Compiler
summary: Compile accepted plans into validated JSON DAGs with dependencies, validation nodes, budget policy, permissions, retries, and timeouts.
acceptanceCriteria: Accepted plans compile to valid DAG JSON with nodes and dependency edges.
Validation, budget, retry, timeout, and tool permission metadata are included.
Unsafe shell permissions are denied by default in compiled metadata.

title: Chunk 13 — Executor Simulator
summary: Run DAGs locally with fake providers and safe node types while enforcing dependencies, retries, timeouts, and shell denial.
acceptanceCriteria: Simulator executes DAG nodes in dependency order with structured results.
Retry policy, timeout metadata, dependency blocking, and unsafe shell denial are enforced in simulation.
No real shell, provider, or external network execution occurs.

title: Chunk 14 — Validation and Healing Loop
summary: Implement bounded self-healing with validation failure classification, safe repair attempts, reruns, and escalation paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Validation failures are classified into deterministic categories.
Safe bounded repairs are proposed and applied only when allowed by policy.
Unresolved or unsafe failures escalate to NEEDS_DECISION or FAILED.

title: Chunk 15 — End-to-End Chat Brainstem Test
summary: Create a full local proof from chat message through triage, planning, review, arbitration, DAG execution, validation, healing, and final answer.
acceptanceCriteria: One chat message drives the complete provider-free orchestration path.
Trace evidence proves triage, context, planning, skeptic, crucible, DAG, execution, validation, and healing steps ran.
Final assistant output summarizes the local trace without requiring external providers.

title: Chunk 16 — Observability Baseline
summary: Add local trace IDs, latency, model-call counts, cost estimates, provider failure events, and no-op telemetry adapter shapes.
acceptanceCriteria: Runs expose trace IDs, span-like timing, model-call count, estimated cost, and structured provider failure events.
Sentry, PostHog, and OpenTelemetry-style adapters are represented as no-op/local shapes.
Tests verify redaction and no external telemetry calls in local mode.

title: Chunk 17 — Provider Adapter Layer Phase 1
summary: Add the LLM provider interface, model router, capability metadata, fake provider contract tests, and opt-in Together AI adapter.
acceptanceCriteria: Provider interface and capability metadata support fake/local and opt-in provider adapters.
Router chooses providers by task needs while preserving provider-free defaults.
Together AI adapter remains disabled unless explicit env and budget gates allow it.

title: Chunk 18 — Provider Adapter Layer Phase 2
summary: Add Cloudflare Workers AI, Azure OpenAI, and Perplexity research adapters with route-based selection and opt-in network behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Cloudflare Workers AI, Azure OpenAI, and Perplexity adapters validate config and build provider requests.
Route-based model assignment covers cheap, fast, flagship, and research calls.
All live-provider behavior remains disabled by default and protected by env and budget gates.

title: Chunk 19 — Memory, Search, and Truth Library
summary: Add local memory/search abstractions and Truth Library statuses with provenance, citations, and rejected-item filtering.
acceptanceCriteria: Truth Library supports TRUSTED, UNVERIFIED, and REJECTED statuses with provenance and citations.
Local search excludes rejected material by default and returns deterministic results.
Chroma and Algolia are represented as optional future integrations, not required local dependencies.

title: Chunk 20 — Public Extension Contracts
summary: Document and test contributor-facing extension contracts for adapters such as LLM, memory, sandbox, telemetry, search, issue tracker, validator, and UI client.
acceptanceCriteria: Public extension manifest and API version compatibility rules are documented.
Adapter contract types cover LLM, memory, sandbox, telemetry, search, issue tracker, validator, and UI client surfaces.
Contributor docs include examples that do not require network access or provider credentials.

title: Chunk 21 — Retool Operator Console API
summary: Expose local operator endpoints for run inspection, failures, approvals, costs, retries, aborts, artifacts, and stub Linear issue creation.
acceptanceCriteria: Operator endpoints expose run, event, cost, artifact, retry, abort, and approval metadata for local inspection.
Endpoints are clearly marked local-only and do not require Retool for tests.
Linear issue creation remains a non-network stub unless future approval and credentials are added.

title: Chunk 22 — Safe Code Execution
summary: Add sandbox adapter contracts, hardened local allowlist behavior, patch artifacts, approval metadata, and E2B/Depot integration paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Sandbox contracts and local safe executor deny arbitrary shell by default.
Patch artifacts and file-write approval metadata are represented without applying unsafe changes automatically.
E2B and Depot adapters are documented/stubbed as opt-in future integrations.

title: Chunk 23 — External Workflow Integrations
summary: Integrate Linear and Make request builders and disabled-by-default invocation gates, with Requestly and BrowserStack plans documented for later.
acceptanceCriteria: Linear and Make payload schemas, config validation, and request builders are tested locally.
Network invocation is disabled by default and protected by explicit configuration gates.
Requestly and BrowserStack integration plans are documented without adding required dependencies.

title: Chunk 24 — Deployment Prototype
summary: Add open-source demo deployment configuration, readiness docs, Heroku and Cloudflare shapes, optional service env examples, and graceful shutdown.
acceptanceCriteria: Deployment configuration parsing validates and redacts Heroku, Cloudflare, MongoDB, Redis, Chroma, Sentry, and PostHog settings.
Deployment docs explain prototype setup without provisioning infrastructure automatically.
Runtime shutdown behavior is graceful and covered by local tests.

title: Chunk 25 — Contributor Issue Breakdown
summary: Convert the roadmap into contributor-ready GitHub issue drafts with labels, acceptance criteria, test commands, difficulty, good-first-issue candidates, and sync guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: Every roadmap chunk has a GitHub issue draft with labels, acceptance criteria, test commands, and difficulty metadata.
Good-first-issue candidates are clearly marked for safe contributor entry points.
Project board and Linear sync guidance is documented as manual and disabled-by-default with no network calls.
The generator can validate committed issue docs with node scripts/generate-roadmap-issues.js --check.
